title: Vendor Registry
description: Canonical schema for a vendor registry. Every vendor the project talks to should have one JSON file in .ai-dev-kit/registries/ conforming to this shape. Registries are the single source of truth for model IDs, pricing, and deprecation patterns. Pre-commit and doctor read them to validate hardcoded strings and flag staleness.

vendor.description: Short lowercase identifier. File name should match (assemblyai.json -> vendor: 'assemblyai').

docs_root.description: Vendor's docs home. The spec-enricher agent fetches from here on `registry refresh`.

console_url.description: Where to get an API key / dashboard login.

validated_on.description: ISO date the registry was last verified against the vendor's current docs. Doctor warns when older than 90 days.

validated_against_sdk.description: Semver range of the SDK this registry was validated against (e.g. 'assemblyai@^4.15.0').

required_env.description: Env vars the vendor's SDK needs. Doctor's Vendor Secrets section cross-references imports to these.

id_patterns.description: Regex patterns that match this vendor's model IDs. The pre-commit scanner compiles these across all registries and flags any source literal that matches a pattern but isn't in the vendor's model lists. Example: ['universal-[\\w-]+', 'u3-[\\w-]+', 'slam-\\d+', 'nano', 'whisper-rt']. Omit and this registry contributes only to explicit validModels() validation, not to the source-literal scanner.

pattern.description: Literal or regex pattern of the deprecated usage (e.g. 'speech_model:' -- the field rename, not a model id).

what.description: What this URL validates (e.g. 'current speech_models values', 'per-model pricing').

^[a-z_]+_models$.description: A 'slot' of models. Name any way that's meaningful for this vendor (chat_models, batch_models, streaming_models, embedding_models, speech_models, tts_models, image_models, video_models, etc). Each entry is a ModelEntry.

id.description: The exact string the vendor's API accepts. This is what goes on the wire.

use_for.description: When to pick this model vs others in the same slot.

pricing.description: Unit-price declaration for non-LLM vendor calls (per-minute transcription, per-page scrape, per-request search, etc.). `withExternalCall` looks this up via vendor-pricing.ts to compute per-call USD attributed to a run_id. The cost-drift checker (scripts/check-cost-drift.mts) re-fetches source_url weekly and fails the run if scraped price drifts by >2%.

usd_per_unit.description: USD charged per unit at the moment of validated_on.

source_url.description: The vendor's pricing page. The cost-drift checker scrapes this.

validated_on.description: ISO date the price was last confirmed.

notes.description: Tier details, volume discounts, commit-terms, etc.

deprecated.description: If true, doctor and registry.ts emit a warning when this ID is used. Still valid for migration-period code.
